lede: A look at October 23 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Niccolò Pisilli and Briana Evigan, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Xi Jinping
events.desc: Xi Jinping is elected as General Secretary of the Chinese Communist Party by the Central Committee, beginning a third term of the paramount leader of China.

events.title: The Troubles
events.desc: The Troubles: A Provisional IRA bomb prematurely detonates in Belfast, killing the bomber and nine civilians.

events.title: Vietnam War
events.desc: Vietnam War: The 1st Cavalry Division (Airmobile), in conjunction with the Army of the Republic of Vietnam, launches an operation seeking to destroy Communist forces during the siege of Plei Me.

events.title: Hungarian Revolution of 1956
events.desc: Secret police shoot several anti-communist protesters, igniting the Hungarian Revolution.

events.title: Saarland
events.desc: The people of the Saar region vote in a referendum to unite with West Germany instead of France.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Allied forces commence the Second Battle of El Alamein, which proves to be the key turning point in the North African campaign.

events.title: Alberto Santos-Dumont
events.desc: Alberto Santos-Dumont flies an airplane in the first heavier-than-air flight in Europe.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: The Battle of Westport is the last significant engagement west of the Mississippi River, ending in a Union victory.

events.title: National Women's Rights Convention
events.desc: The first National Women's Rights Convention begins in Worcester, Massachusetts.

events.title: Battle of Grathe Heath
events.desc: The Battle of Grathe Heath ends the Danish Civil War.

births.name: Anita Roddick
births.line: English businesswoman and activist, founded The Body Shop (died 2007)

deaths.text: Stanley Tanger, American businessman and philanthropist, founded the Tanger Factory Outlet Centers
Adrien de Noailles, 8th Duke of Noailles, French son of Jules Charles Victurnien de Noailles
Georg August Wallin, Finnish explorer, orientalist, and professor

observances.text: Christian feast day: Feast of the Most Holy Redeemer
Christian feast day: James the Just (i.e. James, brother of Jesus) (Lutheran, Episcopal Church (USA), Eastern Orthodox)
Christian feast day: Amon of Toul (Diocese of Toul)
